description_long: This electric drill features a cylindrical metallic body with a smooth, reflective finish, likely made from steel or aluminum. Two robust mounting brackets are attached to the main body, providing stability and support for secure handling or attachment to a surface. The front end of the drill is fitted with a dark, textured chuck, but no drill bit is currently attached. The rear end is capped with a rounded, patterned surface, possibly for grip or ventilation, though no visible slots are present. The overall design is streamlined and functional, with a focus on durability and ease of use in urban environments such as garages or workshops.
description: A metallic, cylindrical electric drill with two mounting brackets and a dark, textured chuck, lacking a visible drill bit.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the drill, both mounting brackets, and the smooth metallic body.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the dark chuck and the symmetrical arrangement of the mounting brackets.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, showing the metallic body and mounting brackets from the other side.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the patterned end cap and the alignment of the mounting brackets.
